Off Calendar Feb-07-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alison Yew, Esq., 415-398-0200, appeared on behalf of defendants and demurring parties. Jean O'Sullivan, 916-565-6222, appeared via CourtCall on behalf of defendants and demurring parties. Ronald D. Schivo, Esq., 415-956-8100, appeared on behalf of the opposing party J. David Enright, IV.

After argument the demurrer was overruled as to the first cause of action for wrongful eviction.

The demurrer was sustained as to the second cause of action for Breach of Warranty of Habitability with 15 days to amend.

As to the third cause of action, wrongful endeavor to recover possession of the premises, the demurrer was overruled.

As to the fourth cause of action for fraud the demurrer was sustained with 15 days to amend.

As to the fifth cause of action for Wrongful Business Practice under B&P 17200 the demurrer was overruled.

At to the sixth cause of action for avoidance of lease the demurrer was sustained with 15 days to amend.

As to the seventh cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress the demurrer was sustained with 15 days to amend.

As to the eighth cause of action for breach of Contract, the ninth cause of action for negligence and the tenth cause of action for common count for money had and received the demurrer was overruled. Hon. James J. McBride. Reporter Eric L. Throne, CSR 7855 (LC)

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Dec-07-2001 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
